title: 谋杀启事
summary:
Miss Blacklock questions why anyone would want to kill her, asserting that she has no enemies and defending her housekeeper, Midge, against suspicion. She suggests that Midge's nervous behavior is merely a reaction to the unsettling newspaper notices rather than an indication of criminal intent.

Inspector Corradino interrogates Midge in the kitchen. Midge claims she locked the front door at 4:00 PM due to the tension surrounding the "terrible advertisement." She disputes Mrs. Hymer's claim that the door was locked at 5:30 PM, instead suggesting that Mrs. Hyde deliberately left it unlocked to assist an accomplice. Midge further alleges that she overheard Mrs. Hyde and Rudolph Scherz talking in the pavilion, where Scherz asked where to hide and Mrs. Hyde replied, "I'll show you," specifying the time "six past one." Midge claims she saw Scherz leave the gazebo and cross the road, and she accuses Mrs. Hyde of being a thief planning to rob Miss Blacklock. While Corradino doubts Midge's reliability, he suspects her testimony may contain some truth.

While moving through the hall, Corradino encounters Miss Bonner, who explains the layout of the house's many doors. He notices a horizontal mark on a wooden doorboard, which Miss Bonner explains is a trace from where a table was previously propped. She notes the table was moved approximately ten to fourteen days ago to accommodate a large floral arrangement by Felicia, an action that also required moving the painting "Wellington at Waterloo" to a corner.
